commands.review_summary:
The PR composes trusted-tool-policy registrations into the global hook-runner registry, passes that composed registry into before_tool_call diagnostics and enforcement, and adds regression coverage for active/pinned registry drift.

PR surface: Source +65, Tests +155. Total +220 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level with high confidence: current main composes live registries for hook dispatch while trusted-policy helpers still read the active registry. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is queued because there is no discrete fixable finding; remaining action is maintainer/security-boundary review of the policy precedence.

target.title: fix: keep trusted policies with hook registry
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix `before_tool_call` trusted-policy lookup so it uses the same composed live plugin registry view as the global hook runner.
- Let trusted-policy helpers read an explicit registry while preserving the active-registry fallback for callers without a global hook registry.
- Preserve trusted-policy tier order across composed registries: bundled policies still run before installed policies.
- Add regression coverage for active-registry drift, later global-runner reinitialization, pinned Gateway registries, and cross-registry policy ordering.

## What Changed
A Gateway run can keep plugin hooks live through the global hook runner while a later scoped/default registry becomes the active registry. Current `main` composes hook dispatch from the latest initialized registry plus live active/pinned registries. Before this change, trusted policies were still read from a single active/latest registry path, so a hook from a pinned Gateway registry could run while that registry's trusted policy was invisible.

The fixed path exposes the same composed live registry view used by global hook dispatch and passes that registry into trusted-policy diagnostics and evaluation.

The composed policy list is still owner-filtered across live registries, then ordered by the existing trusted-policy contract: bundled policies first, installed policies after. That matters because trusted-policy blocks are terminal and policy parameter rewrites feed later policies.

## Behavior Addressed
Tool calls that can still reach plugin `before_tool_call` hooks now evaluate trusted tool policies from the same composed live registry before the ordinary hook path runs.

Trusted policy ordering is preserved even when policies come from different live registries.

| Case | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Pinned Gateway registry has `atl-sec-core`, later registry becomes active | Hook can still run, but trusted policy lookup may miss the pinned registry | Hook and trusted policy use the same composed live registry view |
| Later active registry has an installed policy, pinned registry has a bundled policy | Raw live-source order can put installed policy first | Bundled policy remains first, matching the registry/docs contract |

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: split-registry trusted-policy bypass in `runBeforeToolCallHook(...)`, including the current composed global hook-runner path and trusted-policy ordering across live registries.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on current `origin/main`, branch `jesse/fix-trusted-policy-registry`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/hook-runner-global.test.ts src/plugins/hook-runner-global.compose.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts: 1 file passed, 13 tests passed
src/plugins/hook-runner-global.test.ts + src/plugins/hook-runner-global.compose.test.ts: 2 files passed, 9 tests passed
src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts: 1 file passed, 62 tests passed
core tsgo: passed
source-test tsgo: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

Observed result after fix: the new before-tool-call tests create a Gateway registry with a trusted policy, replace/reinitialize later registries, and confirm the trusted policy still blocks before the ordinary hook runs. The hook-runner-global tests verify the composed registry exposes the pinned Gateway trusted policy after later initialization and keeps bundled policies before installed policies across live registries.

What was not tested: a rebuilt ASSP/RovoClaw container using this patched OpenClaw commit was not run after the code change. The earlier local ASSP debug run reproduced the callback-yes/policy-no symptom before this OpenClaw patch.

## Verification
```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/hook-runner-global.test.ts src/plugins/hook-runner-global.compose.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
passed

git diff --check
passed
```
target.author: jesse-merhi
